steps.title: Identify the verb-position conflict
steps.content: The sentence has Antiquarians ______ to read... followed by ...might procure. The key signal is that might procure is the main verb, so the blank must contain a non-finite verb form.

steps.title: Apply the participle rule
steps.content:
hoping is a present participle that modifies Antiquarians, leaving might procure as the sole main verb.

The rule is: a sentence's main verb must be finite; a participle cannot serve as a main verb.

The alternatives—hope, hoped, have hoped—violate this because they are finite forms that would create a second main verb.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
